mission.title: distribution: In 2.5.1 garbage-collection stops when running into failed to garbage collect: failed to mark: invalid checksum digest format
mission.description: In 2.5.1 garbage-collection stops when running into failed to garbage collect: failed to mark: invalid checksum digest format. Requested by 16+ users.

steps.title: Check current distribution deployment
steps.description:
Verify your distribution version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution
helm list -n distribution 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working distribution installation.

steps.title: Review distribution configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant distribution configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution
kubectl get configmap -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=distribution
```
I guess this is more of a question than a bug, since I assume the issue lies with my environment, but: 
Trying to run garbage-collection, it stops with:
 failed to garbage collect: failed to mark: invalid checksum digest format

Then switched to

steps.title: Apply the fix for In 2.5.1 garbage-collection stops when running into failed…
steps.description:
This issue is due to an empty or corrupted (does not contain a valid digest) tag link file (`/docker/registry/v2/repositories/<name>/_manifests/tags/<tag>/current/link`).

While the root cause is yet unknown, I think it's safe to log a warn message and skip to the next tag instead of stoping GC. By
```yaml
mv data/registry/docker/registry/v2/repositories/some-app data/some-app

docker run -it --name gc --rm -v /opt/registry/data/registry:/var/lib/registry -v /opt/registry/conf/registry/config.yml:/etc/docker/registry/config.yml registry:2.6.2 garbage-collect /etc/docker/registry/config.yml
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n distribution -l app.kubernetes.io/name=distribution
kubectl get events -n distribution --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "In 2.5.1 garbage-collection stops when running into failed…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This issue is due to an empty or corrupted (does not contain a valid digest) tag link file (`/docker/registry/v2/repositories/<name>/_manifests/tags/<tag>/current/link`).

While the root cause is yet unknown, I think it's safe to log a warn message and skip to the next tag instead of stoping GC.
codeSnippets:
mv data/registry/docker/registry/v2/repositories/some-app data/some-app

docker run -it --name gc --rm -v /opt/registry/data/registry:/var/lib/registry -v /opt/registry/conf/registry/config.yml:/etc/docker/registry/config.yml registry:2.6.2 garbage-collect /etc/docker/registry/config.yml

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with distribution installed or the issue environment reproducible.
